title: Monitor-First Attention Model For Follow-Through Insights
summary: Implemented the monitor-first attention model across insight storage, Gmail classification, dashboard/brief rendering, and Telegram delivery. Open threads now persist `attention_mode` and `tracking_key`, monitor items are revisioned separately from act-now debt, and high-signal watch threads no longer surface as direct founder debt.
next_action: Observe real user traffic to tune monitor re-notify thresholds and any remaining Gmail false-positive patterns.

verification.notes: Validated with targeted regression suites, `MIX_ENV=test mix compile --force` to refresh stale test beams, and a final `mix precommit` run with 937 tests passing.

resume_context.last_completed_step: Completed the end-to-end implementation, verification, and Spectacula closeout for the monitor-first attention model.
open_questions: Whether production monitor re-notify behavior needs tighter gating around material-change detection or time-based resurfacing.
resume_context.notes: A stale `_build/test` issue reappeared during verification; forcing a fresh `MIX_ENV=test` compile resolved it before the final green precommit run.

history.note: Initial draft created from observed Gmail false positives, desired monitor-mode behavior, and current repository context.
Implementation began with the schema/storage slice for persisted attention modes and tracking-key-based insight revisioning.
Attention-mode persistence, tracking-key revisioning, monitor-aware Gmail triage, and the dashboard/brief/Telegram surface split shipped and passed precommit.
